specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Can be fulfilled also by completing an industrial internship.

english_requirements.notes: Native speaker or English-language Bachelor's degree, or English as a first language at the German "Abitur" level.

course_content_summary: Master's program in Advanced Quantum Physics including experimental and theoretical courses. Topics cover quantum optics, quantum matter, laser cooling, quantum gases, laser physics, superconductors, Josephson junctions, open and many-body quantum systems, mathematical quantum theory, and quantum information theory. Practical content in quantum labs focuses on lasers, quantum optics elements, superconductors, photons, and photon statistics. Students also choose modules from a neighbouring field, which can be fulfilled by an industrial internship.

program_name: Advisory and Innovation Services in Agri-Food Systems
course_content_summary: Advisory Services, Innovation Services, Agri-Food Systems, Agricultural Sciences, sustainable practices, agricultural production, economics, engineering, social science skills, consulting, farm management, technical specialisation, methodological knowledge, problem-solving, innovation processes, economic factors, decision-making, value chains, agricultural knowledge and innovation systems (AKIS), planning, implementing, evaluating policies, governance, coordination, innovation networks, knowledge systems, knowledge transfer.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: at least 6 ECTS credits combined from microeconomics and statistics

course_content_summary: Research-based and methods-oriented Master's program in Agricultural Economics (AgEcon) focusing on economic, agricultural, and development policy relationships within the agricultural and food economy, environment, and rural areas. Emphasizes economic growth, sustainability, and social justice in international and development contexts. Covers economic and agricultural theories, methodologies, quantitative and qualitative research methods, sustainability integration into policy analysis, interdisciplinary complex systems analysis, communication, and presentation techniques. Prepares for careers in the agri-food sector, regulatory bodies, governmental/non-governmental/international organizations, private sector, teaching, research, and academia.

program_name: Agricultural Sciences in the Tropics and Subtropics (AgriTropics)
course_content_summary: The AgriTropics Master's program focuses on development-oriented agricultural research in tropical and subtropical regions. It addresses challenges like population growth, climate change, and resource depletion, aiming for agroecological, sustainable, and highly productive agriculture. The curriculum provides specific knowledge in tropical agriculture, interdisciplinary approaches, and prepares students to lead multidisciplinary teams. Key skills include assessing natural resource influence on agricultural production, evaluating material cycles (water, nitrogen, carbon), reducing post-harvest losses, promoting innovation in product processing, managing gender-sensitive development and extension approaches, improving marketability of agricultural products, increasing rural incomes, and applying integrated knowledge for sustainable agricultural production in the context of climate change. Graduates find opportunities in international/national agricultural research centers, ministries for development cooperation, land/environmental protection, consulting firms, NGOs, universities, research institutions, agricultural businesses, and EU institutions. The program also qualifies for doctoral studies.

program_name: Air Quality Control, Solid Waste and Waste Water Process Engineering (MSc WASTE)

german_requirements.notes: International students without prior knowledge of German must attend the intensive German language course in September (free of charge).

course_content_summary: The MSc WASTE program focuses on Air Quality Control, Solid Waste, and Waste Water Process Engineering. It covers fundamentals of environmental and process engineering, with advanced modules in the first semester. Students specialize in one of three areas: Air Quality Control, Solid Waste, or Waste Water Process Engineering, each including a core module, elective modules, practical work, or an industrial internship. The curriculum allows for individual study plans, professional profile development, and broadening or specializing studies. It includes excursions, practical work in research facilities, and a Master's thesis. German language courses are mandatory for non-proficient students, with interdisciplinary alternatives for those exempt. A double degree option with UFPR in Brazil is available.

Two-year Master's program in American Studies, offering comprehensive training in methods, theories, literature, cultures, history, and society of the United States. Covers America's colonial past, early literary culture, and contemporary themes including race, class, gender, religious, ethnic, and regional studies. Features an interdisciplinary curriculum emphasizing student autonomy, academic collaboration, and exchange. Core cornerstones are American Literature and Culture, Interdisciplinary Profile, Research, and Practice. Includes an introductory module for research and writing skills, fundamental theoretical frameworks, and guest professors. Students can create unique academic profiles through courses at other departments/faculties, discuss MA thesis projects in a colloquium, and gain professional experience through applied American Studies, organizing exhibitions, festivals, workshops, or tutoring. Encourages practical skills and internships for careers in academia, journalism, publishing, public relations, consulting, international organizations, new media, and public administration.

Focus on resource efficiency, animal welfare, sustainable breeding, husbandry, nutrition, and health of farm animals, and production of high-quality animal foodstuffs. Courses are tailored to job market requirements and current research. Institute of Animal Science focuses on resource-efficient, sustainable, and species-compliant livestock husbandry. Specializations include nutrition and feed, genomics and breeding, health and behaviour. Research focus on microbiota of the digestive tract and its influence on animal and environment. Addresses sustainability, ethical issues, and social, economic, and environmental consequences of agricultural science. Objectives include understanding animal nutrition, animal hygiene, animal behaviour, animal breeding, animal husbandry, anatomy, physiology, endocrinology, genetics, microbiology, feed science, animal husbandry techniques, modern research methods, hypothesis formulation, and critical evaluation of scientific facts.

Modern, interdisciplinary curriculum in geosciences and environmental sciences. Research-oriented program focusing on evaluation and solution of environmental problems, particularly in the subsurface (drinking water pollution, natural resource use, climate and land-use change impact on soil/water quality). Requires comprehensive understanding of physical, chemical, and biological mechanisms. Combines classical natural sciences with modern technology. Covers hydrogeology, environmental modelling, environmental chemistry, environmental microbiology, and environmental physics. Designed for graduate education in environmental geosciences with multidisciplinary approaches. Requires strong background in mathematics, chemistry, and physics. Develops independent problem-solving skills. Specializations available in Hydrogeology, Environmental Chemistry and Environmental Microbiology, and Environmental Physics.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Mathematics, Computer Science, and Artificial Intelligence
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: minimum of 10 ECTS credits in each of these three subject areas

accepted_tests.test_name: ICC (Certificate in English for Business Purposes)

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers exempt. Degree in English accepted. Certificate must not be older than three years at the time of application.

course_content_summary: Applied Artificial Intelligence (MAAI), machine learning, data analysis, artificial neural networks, practical application of AI, healthcare, engineering, logistics, business informatics, software engineering, application development, AI research, two main tracks: Application and Research.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, the required test may be waived.

course_content_summary: International Project Management, Software Architecture, IT Security, Advanced Database, Software Development Practice, Advanced Computer Science, full stack development, front end, middleware, back end, databases, infrastructure, Business Computing, Mobile Computing, Usability Engineering, UX Design, software industry, IT industry, research, doctoral studies.

program_name: Applied Data Science and Artificial Intelligence (MSc)

english_requirements.notes: We may also personally interview applicants in order to test their English skills. If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: Applied Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, business application data scientist, technical courses, statistical courses, big data architectures, data mining algorithms, text mining algorithms, neural networks, predictive analytics, creative questioning techniques, visualisation, data storytelling, ethics, international law, practical experience, case studies, Master's thesis, industry topics.

program_name: Architecture – Design for the Built Environment (MA)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Undergraduate degree (Bachelor's or 'Diplom') in Architecture. If the Bachelor's degree comprises 180 credit points, an additional 30 credit points must be acquired through a preliminary course involving practical experience in an architectural office in Germany and a design project. This preliminary course must be completed by the winter semester for entry into the following summer semester.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, the required test may be waived.

course_content_summary: Reflexive approach to architecture and the city, developing architectural ideas, concepts, and theories, architectural design within urban fabric, project-based teaching, field trips, research projects, academic events, summer schools, conferences.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Knowledge in Astrophysics and Particle Physics
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Proof of knowledge required in these areas.

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers, English-language Bachelor's degree holders, or those with English as the first language at the German 'Abitur' level are exempt.

course_content_summary: International research-oriented Master's program in Astro and Particle Physics at the Kepler Center, Physics Department, University of Tübingen. Focuses on Astronomy, Astrophysics, Astroparticle Physics, and Particle Physics. Combines experimental and theoretical physics, astronomy, and astrophysics. Research areas include origin, structure, and evolution of the universe, and properties of elementary particles under extreme conditions. Coordinated PhD program on Particles, Fields and Messengers of the Universe.

english_requirements.notes: Evidence of very good written and spoken English is required.

course_content_summary: The Biochemistry Master's program at the University of Tübingen focuses on developing laboratory and organisational skills, practical proficiency in research, and personal skills. It offers a broad, flexible, international, and career-oriented curriculum taught in English, including writing and presenting courses. The program supports lab rotations abroad and fosters a worldwide peer network. Core topics include the science of the fundamentals of life and disease, nucleic acids, proteins, lipids, sugars, cells, organisms, and modern drugs derived from biochemical research. It combines biology and chemistry, covering subjects such as immunology, cell biology, molecular and structural biology, biophysics, virology/microbiology, pathobiochemistry, toxicology, pharmacology, bioinformatics, developmental biology, neuroscience, and molecular medicine.

The Master's program in Bioeconomy provides comprehensive education for professionals in sectors utilizing biological resources and biotechnological processes for bio-based goods and services. Students examine the entire value chain of bio-based products, including raw material production in agricultural ecosystems, properties and supply logistics of biological resources, biotechnological and industrial conversion processes, and marketing and consumption. The program addresses environmental, social, and economic dimensions of bioeconomy from micro- and macro-level perspectives, considering innovation, institutions, and policies. As an interdisciplinary program offered by the faculties of Agricultural Sciences, Natural Sciences, and Business, Economics and Social Sciences, it enables systematic analysis of bio-based value chains. Graduates gain expertise to understand bio-based value chains from perspectives of companies focusing on new resources, processes, and products; established producers introducing renewable resources; actors gauging product need and acceptance; and organizations supporting bio-based value chain development through research and advisory services.

german_requirements.notes: Required for Variant B and C students.

course_content_summary: Master's degree in bioinformatics, computer science, life sciences. Focus on solving biological problems using high throughput technologies and bioinformatics methods in interdisciplinary teams. Teaches algorithmic thinking, efficient and advanced methods of analysis, visualisation, mass data storage, with a research and hands-on approach. Subjects include genomics, transcriptomics, protein design, drug design, microbiome analysis, systems biology.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree from countries whose native language is English do not have to prove their English language skills, e.g., USA, Canada, UK, etc.

course_content_summary: The Master's program in Biological Sciences offers a wide variety of advanced courses, allowing students to individually tailor their curriculum. Students can choose a general track (BIOLOGICAL SCIENCES) or priority tracks: CELLULAR & MOLECULAR BIOLOGY, DISEASE BIOLOGY, or ECOLOGY, EVOLUTIONARY & BEHAVIOURAL BIOLOGY. CELLULAR & MOLECULAR BIOLOGY focuses on biochemistry, genetics, and biophysics of macromolecules, preparing for the Konstanz Research School Chemical Biology (KoRS-CB). DISEASE BIOLOGY covers biomedical research, immunology/infections, cell death mechanisms, toxicology, stem cell research, medical microbiology, cancer research, neurodegenerative diseases, biogerontology, molecular and systems biology, cell signalling, and proteostasis. ECOLOGY, EVOLUTION & BEHAVIOUR educates students on research questions focusing on organisms within communities, changing environments, and interactions, including animal behaviour, ecology, evolution, physiology, and neurobiology, with connections to the MPI for Animal Behaviour (IMPRS Quantitative Behaviour, Ecology & Evolution). Teaching occurs in small groups (max 15 students), focusing on research topics and methodological approaches. An internship of at least two months at an external research facility or private sector company is mandatory. All courses are taught in English, aiming to develop independent, self-confident, and highly qualified researchers.

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers exempt if from specified countries (United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, United States of America, Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Dominica, Grenada, Jamaica, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago, Belize, Guyana) OR have completed a Bachelor's or Master's programme with English as the language of instruction at a university in a country listed above or in a member state of the European Union.

course_content_summary: Interdisciplinary, research-oriented Master's program in Biotechnology focusing on properties, production processes, and applications of enzymes and microorganisms in the food industry, supplying industries, and for bioanalytical purposes. Explores enzymatic production processes, biochemical methods including purification, characterisation, immobilisation of enzymes, enzyme kinetics, gene expression, and mutagenesis of recombinant enzymes. Covers pathogens, hygiene in food production, and the importance of utilising microorganisms and enzymes in the life science industry, combining innovation and safe processing.

Functional neuroanatomy, sensory systems, motor systems, neurophysiology, cellular biology of neurons, molecular biology of neurons, cellular biology of glia, molecular biology of glia, neurochemistry, neurotransmitters, neurogenetics, developmental neurobiology, genetic basis of neurological diseases, molecular basis of neurological diseases, genetic basis of psychiatric diseases, molecular basis of psychiatric diseases, neuroglia, microscopy, molecular imaging techniques, tissue culture, tissue engineering, stem cells, laboratory rotations, Master's thesis.

english_requirements.notes: Graduates of English-language Bachelor's study programmes require a certificate from the university from which they obtained their Bachelor's degree (minimum six semesters of study).

course_content_summary: Research-oriented Master's program for international graduates in chemistry or related fields. Offers specialization across broad research areas, advanced hands-on laboratory training, deepening conceptual understanding and analytical skills, and preparation for independent research projects. Flexible structure with four research profiles: Sustainable Synthesis and Catalysis, Smart Materials and Functional Molecules, Biological Chemistry and Biotechnology, Theory and Simulation in Chemistry and Materials Sciences.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree from Australia, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Canada, Malta, New Zealand, Singapore, South Africa or the United States do not have to prove their English language skills.

course_content_summary: Advanced, research-related courses in inorganic chemistry, organic chemistry, physical chemistry. Focus on life sciences and material sciences. Compulsory elective subjects, focus area courses, practical experience, research projects, Master's thesis, doctoral studies preparation.

english_requirements.notes: Higher education degree in a study programme with English as the sole language of instruction; CEFR C1 level or higher shown on the higher education entrance qualification

course_content_summary: Cognitive systems, human cognition, machine intelligence, perception, learning, memory, reasoning, interaction, intelligent robotics, cognitive assistance systems, smart vehicles, smart devices, AI in healthcare, human-computer interaction, psychophysical studies, psychological testing, intelligent technical systems, experiments, simulations, mathematical modelling, computational approaches, theoretical foundations, empirical exploration, development of intelligent systems, improving human action and performance.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Basic knowledge in at least 6 of the following 8 topics, to the extent of at least one module each.

english_requirements.notes: Higher education degree in a study programme with English as the sole language of instruction; CEFR C1 level or higher shown on the higher education entrance qualification

course_content_summary: The Communication and Information Technology Master's program offers specialisations in Communications Circuits and Systems, Sensor Circuits and Systems, Communications Engineering, and Intelligent Systems. Key topics include integrated mixed-signal and RF circuits, RF engineering, radar design, wireless communication, signal processing, optical communication devices, machine learning, modern digital communication methods (coding, networking, advanced modulation), mobile communication systems, microwave/millimetre-wave engineering, microelectronics, optoelectronic devices, integrated sensors, sensor interfaces, pattern recognition, and secure system engineering. The program combines theoretical instruction with hands-on laboratory experience.

program_name: Comparative and Middle East Politics and Society (CMEPS)

english_requirements.notes: Students are exempt from certifying their English language skills under certain conditions (mother tongue, previous study experience in English-language programmes); please consult our homepage for details.

german_requirements.notes: German language skills are not a prerequisite at either of the partner institutions.

course_content_summary: The CMEPS program focuses on Comparative and Middle East Politics and Society. It includes intensive introductory political science courses, research methods, theories of political science, and advanced Arabic language training. Core courses cover Comparative Politics of the Middle East, Regime Change and Democratisation, Comparative Theories of Political Science, and International Cooperation and Development. Elective courses may include international relations, political economy of the Middle East, international migration governance, social movements, development cooperation, conflict analysis, political impact of new media, and political regime analysis. Students also take German classes, complete a study trip, and undertake an internship. The program culminates in a Master's thesis and oral defence.

english_requirements.notes: MoI is not accepted.

course_content_summary: Master of Science in Computational Linguistics (CL) focusing on dialogue systems, machine translation, search engines, and automatic processing of written and spoken language. The program is offered by the Institute for Natural Language Processing Stuttgart, within Faculty 5: Computer Science, Electrical Engineering and Information Technology. It provides advanced education for students from computational linguistics, computer science, general linguistics, and related disciplines. The curriculum includes an intensive review of CL basics, three focus areas (core computational linguistics, applied natural language processing, speech processing), a CL team laboratory for practical skills and collaborative projects, and a CL research module to prepare for Master's theses. The program also offers a wide range of elective modules from the Institute for Natural Language Processing, Computer Science, and Linguistics departments.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a German Abitur including English at least from the year eight to the last year of school with the final grade of "good" and applicants whose native language is English or who graduated from a school at which English was the language of instruction in a country with English as the first official language do not need to submit additional proof of English competence.

course_content_summary: Computational linguistics research, practical skills, grammar formalisms, machine learning, information retrieval, dialectometry, computer-aided language learning, project-driven work, term papers, problem-solving.

program_name: Computational Mechanics of Materials and Structures (COMMAS)
course_content_summary: Computational Mechanics of Materials and Structures (COMMAS), numerical methods, simulation techniques, complex engineering problems, model, understand, predict, validate responses of materials and structures, interdisciplinary, Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, automobile crash tests, failure load prediction, biological tissue modeling, functional materials development, robotics, research-oriented.

Neural data analysis, models of neural coding and computation, physical and physiological bases of neural recordings, brain imaging, computational motor control, computational vision, rehabilitation robotics, brain computer interfaces, systems neuroscience, neurophysiology, basic mechanisms of learning and memory, mathematics, statistics, programming, machine learning for neuroscience, theoretical neuroscience, behaviour and cognition. Includes essay rotation, laboratory rotation, and Master's thesis.

Master's degree in Computer and Information Science focusing on specialization in areas like data science, visualization (visual computing), and human-computer interaction (HCI). Graduates apply and develop practical methods across sectors. The program has a strong research orientation, emphasizing exploratory analysis and visualization of large information spaces. Specialization fields include Data Science, Interactive Systems, Software and Systems, Visual Computing, and Algorithmics. Students receive mentoring from a university professor.

english_requirements.notes: MoI is not accepted.

course_content_summary: Master's degree in Computer Science, English-taught, with specializations in Autonomous Systems in Computer Science (AUT), Service Technology and Engineering (STE), and Visual Computing (VC). Prepares for PhD programs. Obligatory courses are in English, electives may be in German. Focus on research, computer science, engineering, robotics, visual computing, computer engineering, service technology and engineering, database systems, parallel systems, distributed systems. Strong ties to industry (Daimler AG, Porsche, Bosch, Hewlett-Packard, IBM, Philips, Lucent Alcatel, Agilent) and academia.

program_name: Computer Science for Industry and Business (MSc COSIB)

english_requirements.notes: Evidence of five years of continuous English classes, level German secondary school up to Abitur (German equivalent to A-level), minimum grade "befriedigend" or better; Successful completion of a minimum of four subject-related courses held in English in a previous relevant degree programme or exchange programme (ERASMUS, etc.) in the European Economic Area (EEA) or Switzerland; Successful completion of a minimum of three subject-related courses in English and the writing of the final thesis in English in a previous relevant degree programme in the European Economic Area (EEA) or Switzerland; High school or Bachelor's degree or study exchange certificate (min. one semester) from an English educational institution from one of the following countries: USA, UK, Canada, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa

course_content_summary: The one-year Master's programme in Computer Science for Industry and Business (MSc COSIB) offers compact further qualification in computer science, combining theoretical and practical aspects with a career-oriented focus. It targets students with a first academic degree (at least 240 ECTS / four-year degree) in a STEM subject and at least one year of professional work experience, seeking reorientation or deepening of subject skills in computer science. Students develop an in-depth understanding of basic principles including algorithms, data structures, and programming languages, learning to design, analyse, and implement efficient algorithms. The program allows for specialisations through profiling specialist modules, including a Data Science profile. This profile covers comprehensive knowledge in data analysis, processing, and visualisation, particularly in big data and machine learning, statistical methods, and various machine learning approaches. The program has a strong practice-oriented component, preparing students for the IT labour market in Germany, including an integrated eight-week compulsory internship and opportunities for Master's thesis in a company, alongside career services.

english_requirements.notes: Degree in English accepted
If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, the required test may be waived.

course_content_summary: Dance Movement Therapy (DMT) is an evidence-based approach using dance and movement for physical, emotional, cognitive, social, and spiritual integration. It's an arts therapy utilizing creative methods for self-expression, social connection, and emotional well-being. Applied in research and clinical practice, DMT is an important addition to standard care. Professional contexts include psychiatry, psychosomatic medicine, special education, neurology, oncology, cardiovascular medicine, preventive care, geriatrics, forensic contexts, paediatrics, intensive medicine centres, psychosocial institutions for children and families with special needs, disabilities, early intervention, and institutions of prevention and education (schools, youth offers, kindergartens).

program_name: Development, Environment, Societies, and History of South Asia

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers from majority English-speaking countries are exempt. Bachelor's degree earned in a majority English-speaking country is accepted as proof.

german_requirements.notes: Not needed for program completion. Passive knowledge of German enhances access to university offerings, student services, and life in Germany.

course_content_summary: Two-year Master's program at Heidelberg University's South Asia Institute, taught in English. Focuses on interdisciplinary research in historical, social, current political, economic developments, geography, and environment across South Asia (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka). Cultivates independent work and self-reflexivity through humanities and social sciences paradigms. Students select study focuses from Political Science, History, Anthropology, Art History and Visual Culture, Development Economics, and Geography. Curriculum includes guest professorships and open talks. Offers instruction in South Asian languages: Bengali, Dari, Hindi, Classical Tibetan, Nepali, Pali, Sanskrit, Sinhala, Tamil, and Urdu.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Including 5 ECTS in Statistics. A preparatory course (StatUpgrade) is offered if Statistics ECTS are missing.
Total of 30 ECTS from Business Administration and/or Economics.

english_requirements.notes: Degree in English accepted

course_content_summary: The Master's in Digital Business Management (MDBM) focuses on digital transformation projects, Digital Management, Digital Business Models, Products, Project and Process Management, Algorithms, and Data Analytics. The program provides profound understanding and in-depth knowledge of digital transformation, covering process analytics, process design, data analytics, algorithms, and responsible management methods. Students gain applied skills in project management methods, techniques, tools, and leadership training, with a research-based view on current developments. Elective courses allow for individual profile shaping in contemporary management, strategic management, and leadership. The program emphasizes high employability, a strong network with companies in an economically strong region, and interactive teaching through case studies, company projects, and empirical studies. Pforzheim Business School is AACSB accredited.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants are exempted if English is their mother tongue and they are citizens of specific English-speaking countries; or have completed their Bachelor's studies with English as the language of instruction; or have visited a bilingual school with English as one language of instruction; or have taken English as a school subject during the Oberstufe at a German Gymnasium with a minimum grade point average of 'befriedigend' (eight grade points).

course_content_summary: Interdisciplinary and research-oriented Master's program focusing on Earth and Climate System Science. Covers complex processes in the Earth's environment, including population growth, climate change, land use, food production, and food security. Integrates natural sciences with agricultural and economic sciences. Students acquire understanding of key Earth system processes, human activities, and learn to assess the Earth system's current status through indicator analysis. Skills include simulating Earth system components (e.g., regional climate) with a focus on land surface, land use, land management, and agricultural activities. Essential knowledge for end users and decision-makers in climate change era. Develops concepts of sustainability for environmental protection, food security, and guiding public and decision-makers.

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers exempt

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in Economics at the University of Konstanz focuses on modern scientific methods of economics and their application to economic problems. Students can specialize in Econometrics and Applied Economics, International Financial Economics, Macroeconomics and International Economics, Microeconomics and Decision-Making, and Public Economics. The program emphasizes close contact with professors and provides a firm background in economic theory and quantitative methods.

program_name: Economics (MSc) with Double Degree Option with the University of Nottingham

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Sound intermediate knowledge required in all listed areas.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur", including at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English; Bachelor's degree in English/American Studies; University degree obtained in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand. The University of Nottingham requires an IELTS of 6.5 (no less than 6.0 in any element) or an equivalent qualification for the double degree option.

german_requirements.notes: German is not required. However, German classes are available.

course_content_summary: The MSc Economics program offers state-of-the-art education in economics, preparing students for careers in academia, public, and private sectors. Key topics include Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Quantitative Methods (Statistics, Mathematics, Econometrics), ICT Adoption on Firm Organisation, Heterogenous Agent Macroeconomics, International Taxation, Economics of Taxation, Fiscal Policy during the Covid Pandemic, Advanced Macroeconomics, Computational Macroeconomics, Spillovers of war, Empirical Topics in International Trade. The program emphasizes cutting-edge research, small class sizes, close supervision, and research assistantships, leveraging the university's excellence in research and its world-leading machine learning cluster.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur", including at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English or Bachelor's degree in English/American Studies or University degree obtained in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand

german_requirements.notes: Knowledge of German is not required to complete the programme.

course_content_summary: The MSc in Economics and Finance addresses the consequences of the 2008 financial crisis, focusing on economic theory, financial instruments for risk management, and modern econometric analysis. Graduates acquire skills to analyze financial and economic data using econometric and statistical software (Matlab, GAUSS, SAS, Stata), understand risky asset pricing, and the role of financial instruments like options and futures. The program emphasizes methodological skills (theoretical, econometric, programming) for careers in private/public financial institutions or academia.

Electrical engineering, information technology, international environment, industrial centres, specialisation areas: Smart Information Processing, Communication Systems, Nano- and Optoelectronics, Power-Electronic Systems and Technologies, Smart Systems, Electromagnetics and Applications, Electrical Energy Systems. Focus on research funding, industry connections, academic studies, and professional pursuits.

Engineering Cybernetics, methodologically-oriented courses, Control, Robotics, System dynamics, Modelling and simulation, foundational courses, applied mathematical tools, abstracting complex problems, traditional and modern engineering applications, aerospace, mechanical, biomedical, energy systems, control analysis, design problems, systems theory, dynamical systems modelling, simulation, computational methods.

program_name: Environmental Protection and Agricultural Food Production (EnviroFood)
course_content_summary: The Environmental Protection and Agricultural Food Production (EnviroFood) Master's program focuses on sustainable environmental protection and intensifying food production with environmentally friendly and sustainable systems. It considers ecological, economic, political, and administrative factors within agroecosystems. Students acquire knowledge in analyzing physical, biological, and chemical properties of environmental chemicals, model development for environmental systems, conducting simulations, analyzing geodata using geographic information systems (GIS), and developing integrative, global problem-solving approaches. Graduates are qualified for careers in environmental management, quality management, agricultural and environmental consulting, research and development in national and international companies and organizations, and research institutions. The program also prepares students for doctoral projects.

program_name: Environmental Science – Soil, Water and Biodiversity (EnvEuro)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Proven knowledge in at least two of these areas required.

course_content_summary: European double Master’s program in Environmental Science focusing on sustainable use and management of natural resources in Europe. Specializations include Water Resources, Soil Resources and Land Use, Ecosystems and Biodiversity, Environmental Management, and Climate Change. Students study at two Euroleague for Life Sciences universities. The program aims to teach principles of environmental management of ecosystems, identify problems in natural resource management, develop solutions in an international context, and foster international collaboration. Graduates become experts in environmental sciences, qualified for careers in environmental and quality management, environmental technology, consultancy for agriculture, landscape planning, environmental protection, impact assessment, risk analysis, research and development, research institutions, and government agencies. It also qualifies for doctorate programs.

program_name: ESB Business School: Global Management and Digital Competencies (Master of Science)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Minimum 210 ECTS from a three-and-a-half year program. Applicants with 180 ECTS (from a three-year Bachelor's programme) have to complete an additional internship during the programme (relevant work experience post graduation may be pre-accredited instead).
Graduates of non-business degree programmes must have taken at least four courses in business, including two from the quantitative area.

english_requirements.notes: TOEFL: minimum of 21 speaking & writing; IELTS: minimum of 6 per unit; Pearson Test of English (Academic): minimum 50 in each component.

course_content_summary: Global Management, Digital Competencies, international study, partner universities (France, Ireland, Italy, Canada), Reutlingen, thesis, internship, intercultural teams, interdisciplinary teams, academic experience, professional experience, practical orientation, corporate world, theoretical teaching, best practice, digital disruption, global business, management, global top management, AACSB International accreditation.

english_requirements.notes: Bachelor’s degree conducted in English accepted as proof.

course_content_summary: The AACSB accredited, full-time ESB MBA program focuses on international management, career advancement in Germany/Europe, and industry-relevant experience. It features small, interactive classes with diverse international students and instructors with industry experience. The core program includes six clusters of key management subjects and intensive German language courses. The second semester involves further German language development, academic writing, business research methods, and a choice of four out of ten elective subjects taught by industry experts. A mandatory three-month in-company project/placement and a three-month thesis are integral, allowing students to apply knowledge to real-world business challenges in companies ranging from start-ups to multi-national enterprises. Career trajectories include management positions in marketing, product management, business/strategy development, key account management, controlling, consultancy, international/global sales, international trade, global procurement, logistics, fulfilment, tech start-up development, and supply chain management.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur" including at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English. Officially recognised higher education degree (e.g. Bachelor's degree) with English as the language of instruction (You must provide a medium of instruction certificate stating that the programme was conducted in English, unless the language of instruction is definitively stated on your transcript!). The University of Nottingham requires an IELTS of 6.5 (no less than 6.0 in any element) or an equivalent qualification.

german_requirements.notes: German is not required. However, German classes are available.

course_content_summary: European Economics, double degree, graduate level economics education, integrated year of study, international focus, European economic world, international companies, public sector, Business Administration, Economics, basic principles, theories and concepts in the field of economics, Microeconomics, Trade Theory and Policy, Finance, Quantitative Methods (Mathematics / Statistics / Econometrics).
